title: CIL Geology — Full-length Mock 07
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.solution: Anthracite

questions.solution: 5 L

questions.stem: If price of sugar increases by 20%, by what percent must consumption reduce to keep expenditure same?

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Statements: All papers are files. Some files are folders. All folders are covers. Conclusions: I. Some covers are files. II. Some folders are papers.
questions.solution: Only I follows

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Ravi walks 4 km south, 3 km east, 4 km north. Shortest distance from start:
questions.solution: 3 km

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the wrong number: 5, 15, 30, 80, 160, 480

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: research / this / significant / has / implications / study / for
options: This study has significant implications for research
Significant implications this study has for research
Research this study has significant implications for
For research this study has significant implications
questions.solution: This study has significant implications for research

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of PLACID:
questions.solution: Stormy

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: sustainable / practices / mining / promote / must
options: Sustainable development must promote mining practices
Mining practices must promote sustainable development
Must promote sustainable development mining practices
Practices mining sustainable must promote development
questions.solution: Mining practices must promote sustainable development

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of HARMONY:
questions.solution: Discord

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: performance / regular / improves / employee / training
questions.solution: Regular training improves employee performance

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of TRANQUIL:
questions.solution: Agitated

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: corruption / development / hinders / economic
questions.solution: Corruption hinders economic development

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of AUDACIOUS:
questions.solution: Meek

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: climate / urgent / change / requires / action / addressing
options: Addressing climate change requires urgent action
Climate change addressing urgent action requires
Requires urgent action change addressing climate
Urgent action climate change requires addressing
questions.solution: Addressing climate change requires urgent action

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of VENERATE:
questions.solution: Despise

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: government / new / the / announced / policy / has / a / mining
options: The government has a new mining policy announced
Government has the new mining policy announced
The government has announced a new mining policy
Announced a new mining policy the government has
questions.solution: The government has announced a new mining policy

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of AMICABLE:
questions.solution: Hostile

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: plays / role / coal / in / energy / a / vital / production / India
options: Coal plays a vital role in India energy production
Vital role coal plays in energy production India
In India coal plays a energy vital production role
Coal in India plays vital role energy production
questions.solution: Coal plays a vital role in India energy production

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of ALLEVIATE:
questions.solution: Aggravate

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: mining / India / in / industry / is / a / important / sector
options: An important sector in India is mining industry
Mining industry is a important sector in India
Mining is an important sector in India
In India mining is important sector
questions.solution: Mining is an important sector in India

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Antonym of PROCRASTINATE:
questions.solution: Hasten

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: workers / the / demanded / conditions / working / better
options: Demanded the workers better working conditions
Working conditions demanded the workers better
The workers demanded better working conditions
Better working conditions the workers demanded
questions.solution: The workers demanded better working conditions

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Correct: She is the tallest of the two sisters.
options: She is the taller of the two sisters.
She is the tallest of the two sisters.
She is more tall of the two sisters.
She is tall among the two sisters.
questions.solution: She is the taller of the two sisters.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: He told (A) / the news (B) / to his (C) / brother and I. (D)
questions.solution: D - should be 'me'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Rearrange: country / infrastructure / of / development / backbone / the / is
options: The backbone of country development is infrastructure
Development of country backbone is infrastructure
Country development backbone is the infrastructure
Infrastructure is the backbone of country development
questions.solution: Infrastructure is the backbone of country development

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: He is (A) / more cleverer (B) / than his (C) / brother. (D)
questions.solution: B - should be 'cleverer' or 'more clever'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of PERILOUS:
questions.solution: Hazardous

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Find the error: The committee (A) / have submitted (B) / its report (C) / to the board. (D)
questions.solution: B - should be 'has submitted'

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of COPIOUS:
questions.solution: Abundant

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Synonym of DILIGENT:
questions.solution: Industrious

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Bearing capacity of rocks:
options: Based on UCS and rock mass quality. Safe bearing capacity = UCS/FOS
questions.solution: BC: higher for massive granite (>10 MPa), lower for shale (<1 MPa). Plate load test.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Shear sense — mica fish:
options: Lozenge-shaped mica in mylonite. Tails extend in shear direction. Asymmetric shape = shear sense
questions.solution: Mica fish: bookshelf sliding. Fractured mica flakes. Chlorite-mica fish.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hardness of gypsum:
questions.solution: Gypsum (CaSO4·2H2O) — hardness 2. Can be scratched by fingernail.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Marble is the metamorphic product of:
questions.solution: Limestone recrystallizes into marble under metamorphism.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An opencast block of 20000 m² has 30 m of overburden above the seam. The overburden volume to be removed is:
questions.solution: Volume = area × depth = 20000×30 = 600000 m³ = 0.6 million m³.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Wilson cycle:
options: Rifting → Spreading → Subduction → Collision (orogeny). Complete cycle of ocean opening and closing
questions.solution: Wilson cycle: Atlantic (opening), Tethys (closing → Himalaya). Supercontinent cycle.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Pangaea assembly:
questions.solution: Pangaea assembled ~335-299 Ma.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Palynology: palynomorphs include:
options: Pollen, spores, dinoflagellate cysts, acritarchs, fungal spores
questions.solution: Palynomorphs: acid-resistant organic microfossils. Used for biostratigraphy, paleoenvironment.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Trilobite extinction occurred at end of:
questions.solution: Trilobites: appeared Cambrian, diversified through Paleozoic, extinct at Permian-Triassic extinction ~252 Ma.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lateritic nickel (Ni-laterite):
options: Ultramafic rocks weathered → Ni concentrated in saprolite and limonite. Goro, Murrin Murrin
questions.solution: Ni-laterite: New Caledonia, Indonesia. Limonite (Fe-Ni) + saprolite (Mg-Ni silicate).

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Isochron method in Rb-Sr dating determines:
questions.solution: Isochron: slope = e^(λt) - 1 gives age, intercept = initial 87Sr/86Sr. Multiple minerals plotted.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Quartzite:
options: Metamorphosed sandstone. Quartz grains recrystallized. Very hard, resistant
questions.solution: Quartzite: fracture through grains (not around). Orthoquartzite: >95% quartz.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Kelyphitic rim around garnet indicates:
questions.solution: Kelyphite: symplectite rim (pyroxene + spinel + plagioclase). Forms when garnet becomes unstable during decompression.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: K-Pg extinction (~66 Ma):
options: Chicxulub impact. 75% species extinct (non-avian dinosaurs). Iridium anomaly
questions.solution: K-Pg: Deccan Traps + impact. Alvarez hypothesis. Iridium layer worldwide. Mammals radiated.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Oxbow lake is formed by:
questions.solution: Oxbow: cutoff meander loop. Neck cutoff or chute cutoff. Becomes isolated lake, eventually fills with sediment.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rockfall — talus cone:
options: Accumulation of fallen rock at cliff base. Angle of repose 35-40°
questions.solution: Talus: scree. Grain size decreases downslope. Rockfall shadow: probabilistic runout.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Strain analysis — Fry method:
options: Center-to-center distance of grains. Finite strain ellipse from initially random distribution
questions.solution: Fry: nearest neighbor method. Rf/φ: deformed elliptical objects (ooliths, pebbles).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Threshold hillslope model: slope angle controlled by:
options: Rock strength and weathering rate, not just uplift rate
questions.solution: Threshold slopes: maximum stable angle. Uplift > erosion → steepening until failure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Layered mafic intrusions:
options: Bushveld (S. Africa), Stillwater (USA), Skaergaard (Greenland). Cyclic units. Cumulus phases
questions.solution: Layered: rhythmic layering, graded, inch-scale. Chromitite (Bushveld). PGE reefs (Merensky, J-M).

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Water table in unconfined aquifer:
questions.solution: Water table: surface where P = Patm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Trigonal system is subdivision of:
questions.solution: Trigonal: rhombohedral lattice. Quartz, calcite, garnet. 3-fold symmetry.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Joint — cooling joints:
options: Columnar joints in basalt. Polygonal (hexagonal). Perpendicular to cooling surface
questions.solution: Columnar: Giant's Causeway. Cooling contraction → tensile fractures. ≤1m diameter columns.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Earth's magnetic field: geodynamo:
options: Convection in liquid outer core. Dynamo action generates dipole field
questions.solution: Geodynamo: convection of liquid iron in outer core. Self-sustaining. Reverses periodically.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cuddapah Supergroup:
options: Proterozoic (1.6-0.6 Ga). Vindhyan also. Cuddapah: Pb-Zn deposits (Zawar)
questions.solution: Cuddapah: Papaghni, Chitravati groups. Vindhyan: Semri, Kaimur, Rewa, Bhander.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Storage coefficient of confined aquifer:
questions.solution: S = Ss * b. Very small for confined (elastic storage).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Space group: combination of point group symmetry + translational symmetry:
options: 32 point groups × 14 Bravais lattices + glide planes + screw axes = 230 space groups
questions.solution: Space group: complete description of crystal symmetry. International notation: e.g., Fd3̄m (diamond).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: U-Pb concordia diagram: discordant zircons plot below concordia due to:
options: Lead loss during metamorphism or weathering
questions.solution: Discordia: mixing line between original age and Pb loss event. Upper intercept = crystallization age.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Alluvial fan facies:
options: Coarse, poorly sorted. Proximal (debris flow) to distal (sheet flood, sieve)
questions.solution: Alluvial fan: arid/semiarid. Debris flows, stream channels. Bajada: coalescing fans.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Landsat 8 thermal band (Band 10):
options: TIRS (Thermal Infrared Sensor). Surface temperature measurement
questions.solution: Landsat 8: Band 10 (10.6-11.19 um). 100m resolution (resampled to 30m).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 3 m seam over 4 km² (density 1.4 t/m³) has a recovery factor of 0.85. The mineable reserve is about:
questions.solution: Mineable = area×thickness×density×recovery = 4M m²×3×1.4×0.85 ≈ 14.3 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Transform plate boundary: relative motion of plates is:
questions.solution: Transform: conservative. No creation/destruction of lithosphere. San Andreas Fault.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A mineral has passed through 3 half-lives of an isotope whose half-life is 4.5 (in the given time units). Its age is:
questions.solution: Age = number of half-lives × half-life = 3×4.5 = 13.5.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Oxide copper resources amenable to:
questions.solution: Oxide Cu: soluble in sulfuric acid. Heap leach or in-situ.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: At a station the S-wave arrives 20 s after the P-wave. Using ~8 km per second of S–P delay, the epicentral distance is about:
questions.solution: distance ≈ (S−P time) × 8 km/s = 20×8 = 160 km.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seismic refraction: requires velocity to increase with depth.
options: First arrivals always from faster layer below
questions.solution: Refraction: requires V2 > V1. If velocity decreases, no refraction arrival. Shadow zone.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Seismic velocity: P-wave vs S-wave:
options: P: compressional, faster (~6 km/s in crust). S: shear, slower (~3.5 km/s in crust)
questions.solution: P: pushes/pulls. S: shears. P/S ratio: granite ~1.7, sandstone ~1.6-1.8. S can't travel through liquid.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Which statement comparing seismic waves is correct?
options: S-waves cannot travel through liquids, but P-waves can
questions.solution: Shear (S) waves need a solid; compressional (P) waves pass through solids, liquids and gases, and are faster.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A P-wave travels at 7 km/s and arrives 20 s after the earthquake. The (approx.) distance to the source is:
questions.solution: distance = velocity × time = 7×20 = 140 km.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Mineral exploration: geophysical methods for massive sulfides:
questions.solution: Massive sulfides: conductive (EM), polarizable (IP), magnetic (pyrrhotite, magnetite).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Paleomagnetic dating:
options: Magnetic polarity reversals. GPTS (geomagnetic polarity time scale). Correlation of magnetozones
questions.solution: GPTS: Cande and Kent. Chron C1n to C34. Polarity chrons, subchrons. Marine magnetic anomalies.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Foliation:
options: Planar fabric in metamorphic rocks (cleavage, schistosity, gneissic banding)
questions.solution: Foliation: preferred orientation of minerals. Parallel to axial plane of folds.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A 6 m seam over 2 km² (density 1.4 t/m³) has a recovery factor of 0.75. The mineable reserve is about:
questions.solution: Mineable = area×thickness×density×recovery = 2M m²×6×1.4×0.75 ≈ 12.6 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An aquifer has hydraulic conductivity 10 m/day and a hydraulic gradient of 0.05. The Darcy velocity is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Zeolite group:
options: Hydrated alumino-silicates. Open framework. Used as molecular sieves
questions.solution: Zeolites: large cavities with H2O. Ion exchange, catalysis. Natural and synthetic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Spheroidal weathering:
options: Corners of joint blocks rounded by chemical attack. Concentric shells
questions.solution: Spheroidal: in jointed rocks. Water penetrates joints, attacks from all sides. Corestones.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Spatial interpolation — IDW:
options: Inverse Distance Weighted. Closer points have more influence. p = power parameter
questions.solution: IDW: d^-p. p=2 common. No uncertainty estimate. Kriging: geostatistical, best linear unbiased.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Earthquake waves:
options: P-wave (compressional, first). S-wave (shear, damaging). Surface waves (Love, Rayleigh)
questions.solution: P: 5-7 km/s in crust. S: 3-4 km/s. Surface waves: most destructive (slow, large amplitude).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Peralkaline rhyolite: molar (Na₂O+K₂O)/Al₂O₃ > 1. Mineralogy:
options: Sodium-rich amphibole (riebeckite), aegirine, no primary muscovite
questions.solution: Peralkaline: excess alkalis over Al. Riebeckite, aegirine. Pantellerite (Fe-rich), comendite.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Dharwar lithology:
questions.solution: Dharwar: Sargur Group (3.4-3.0 Ga) + Dharwar Supergroup (3.0-2.5 Ga).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sedimentary rock color: red = iron oxide (hematite, oxidizing). Gray = reducing conditions. White =:
questions.solution: White: clean quartz sandstone, pure limestone, kaolinite. Absence of coloring agents.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Metasomatism:
options: Change in bulk composition by fluid transport of elements
questions.solution: Metasomatism: addition/removal of elements. Skarn: Ca-Fe-Mg alteration of carbonate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Well logging — sonic:
options: Compressional slowness Δt (μs/ft). Porosity: ϕ = (Δt - Δt_ma)/(Δt_f - Δt_ma) (Wyllie). Secondary porosity indicator
questions.solution: Sonic: Δt_ma: 55.5 (ss), 47.5 (ls), 43.5 (dol). Crossplot with density → lithology.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Sedimentary exhalative (SEDEX) deposits form at:
questions.solution: SEDEX: Zn-Pb from hydrothermal fluids on seafloor.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: U-Pb dating of zircon:
options: ²³⁸U → ²⁰⁶Pb (T½ = 4.47 Ga). ²³⁵U → ²⁰⁷Pb (T½ = 704 Ma). Concordia diagram. Discordia = Pb loss
questions.solution: U-Pb: robust dating. Concordia: both decay systems agree. Discordia: Pb loss event. SHRIMP, LA-ICPMS.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a plunging anticline eroded to a level surface, the outcrop pattern of beds forms a:
options: V or nose closing in the direction of plunge
questions.solution: Plunging folds produce V-shaped (zig-zag) outcrops; an anticline's nose points in the plunge direction.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: To extract 5 m³ of coal, 30 m³ of overburden must be removed. The stripping ratio is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 30/5 = 6 : 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Darcy velocity vs seepage velocity:
questions.solution: Darcy velocity: apparent. Seepage velocity: actual through pores. v_s = v/n_e (effective porosity).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Interference colors in quartz:
questions.solution: Quartz: low birefringence → grey-white colors under crossed polars. Uniaxial positive.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Erosion rate: universal soil loss equation (USLE): A = R*K*L*S*C*P
options: R=rainfall erosivity, K=soil erodibility, L=slope length, S=slope steepness
questions.solution: USLE: A=RKLSCP. R: rainfall energy. K: soil type. L,S: topography. C: cover. P: practice.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Radar backscatter increases with:
questions.solution: Radar: rough surfaces scatter more energy back.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Landslide remediation: rock bolts provide:
options: Active reinforcement by compressing rock mass and increasing frictional resistance
questions.solution: Rock bolts: grouted or mechanical. Passive: resist movement. Active: pre-tensioned to compress.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: IUGS classification — plutonic:
options: QAPF diagram. Q = quartz, A = alkali feldspar, P = plagioclase, F = feldspathoid
questions.solution: IUGS: QAPF for felsic. Ultramafic: olivine-pyroxene-hornblende. Volcanic: TAS diagram.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Evaporites:
questions.solution: Evaporites: form in restricted basins (high evaporation). Economic: salt, plaster.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Borehole logging: sonic (acoustic) log Δt:
options: Slowness (μs/ft) inversely related to velocity. Porosity indicator
questions.solution: Sonic: Δt = 1/V. Wyllie equation: porosity from Δt. Compaction correction needed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Three-point problem:
options: Determine strike and dip from three outcrop points. Elevation differences
questions.solution: Three-point: graphical or algebraic. Strike from equal elevation points. Dip from elevation difference.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Water table vs potentiometric surface:
options: Water table: phreatic surface (unconfined). Potentiometric: head in confined aquifer
questions.solution: Water table: actual water level in unconfined aquifer. Potentiometric: imaginary surface in confined aquifer.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Spatial analysis — overlay:
options: Intersection of multiple layers. Weighted overlay for suitability
questions.solution: Overlay: slope + geology + land use for site suitability. Boolean or fuzzy logic.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Vindhyan Basin:
options: Proterozoic (~1.7-0.5 Ga). Largest Proterozoic basin in India. Semri + Kaimur + Rewa + Bhander
questions.solution: Vindhyan: undeformed/thrusted margins. Bhander: stromatolitic limestone. Son Valley.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A bed dipping at 60° shows a width of 20 m across a horizontal surface. Its true (perpendicular) thickness is about:
questions.solution: True thickness = width × sin(dip) = 20×sin60° ≈ 17.32 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Badland topography:
options: Intense gully erosion in soft rocks (shale, clay). High drainage density. Steep slopes, rapid erosion rates
questions.solution: Badlands: South Dakota, Spiti Valley. Land use limitation. Gully rehabilitation needed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Red beds versus gray beds:
options: Red: hematite coating (oxidizing). Gray: pyrite/organic matter (reducing)
questions.solution: Red beds: continental (fluvial, aeolian). Gray: marine or deltaic. Diagenetic color.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stream ordering — Strahler method:
options: Headwaters = order 1. Two of same order → next order. Mainstream = highest order
questions.solution: Strahler: no increase at confluence of different orders. Bifurcation ratio: R_b = N_u/N_{u+1}.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Organic-rich shale (black shale):
options: High TOC (>2%). Reducing conditions. Hydrocarbon source rock
questions.solution: Black shale: anoxic. Pyrite framboids. Total organic carbon. Barnett, Marcellus shales.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Q-system formula: Q = (RQD/Jn)×(Jr/Ja)×(Jw/SRF). For RQD=75, Jn=12, Jr=1.5, Ja=2, Jw=1, SRF=1:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Geological considerations for tunnels:
options: Rock mass quality, groundwater inflow, swelling/squeezing ground, gas
questions.solution: Tunnels: avoid fault zones. TBM vs drill-and-blast. Rock support: bolts, shotcrete, steel.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal seam covers 2 km², is 2 m thick, with density 1.4 t/m³. The in-situ reserve is about:
questions.solution: Reserve = area×thickness×density = 2×10⁶ m²×2 m×1.4 t/m³ ≈ 5.6 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An upward-arching fold, with the oldest beds in its core, is called a(n):
questions.solution: An anticline arches upward; its limbs dip away from the axis and oldest rocks lie in the core.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A coal seam covers 5 km², is 5 m thick, with density 1.4 t/m³. The in-situ reserve is about:
questions.solution: Reserve = area×thickness×density = 5×10⁶ m²×5 m×1.4 t/m³ ≈ 35 Mt.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Apparent dip calculation:
options: tan α = cos δ · tan θ
tan α = tan δ · cos θ
tan α = sin δ · cos θ
tan α = tan δ · sin θ. α = apparent dip, δ = true dip, θ = angle between strike and section
questions.solution: Apparent dip is always ≤ true dip. Used for cross-sections not perpendicular to strike.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Streak of hematite:
questions.solution: Hematite (Fe2O3) — reddish streak. Important iron ore.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Darcy's law states that groundwater discharge is proportional to the hydraulic gradient and the:
questions.solution: Q = K·i·A; flow rises with hydraulic conductivity K and gradient i.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section shows 30 m of overburden above a 3 m coal seam. The volumetric stripping ratio (overburden : coal thickness) is:
questions.solution: Stripping ratio = overburden/coal = 30/3 = 10 : 1.

figure.caption: Coal seam with overburden

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Flinn diagram: K = (R-1)/(R-1) where R = axial ratios. K>1 indicates:
questions.solution: K>1: elongation dominant (L-tectonite). K<1: flattening dominant (S-tectonite). K=1: plane strain.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: On the Mohs scale of hardness, the hardest mineral (value 10) is:
questions.solution: Mohs scale runs from talc (1) to diamond (10), the hardest natural mineral.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A rock retains 50% of its original parent isotope (half-life 4.47 Gyr). Its radiometric age is about:
questions.solution: 50% remaining ⇒ 1 half-lives ⇒ age = 1×4.47 = 4.47 Gyr.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Liquefaction potential is highest in:
questions.solution: Liquefaction: saturated loose sand loses strength during shaking. High water table + loose sand + earthquake = high risk.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Geological considerations for dam sites:
options: Foundation on competent rock, watertight abutments, no active faults
questions.solution: Dam: avoid karst (leakage), shears, soluble rocks. Grout curtain for seepage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Arroyo:
options: Steep-walled, flat-floored ephemeral channel in arid regions. Incised
questions.solution: Arroyo: incision by base level fall or climate change. SW USA. Valley fill.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Back-arc basin formation: extension behind volcanic arc:
options: Caused by rollback of subducting slab or mantle wedge convection
questions.solution: Back-arc: Sea of Japan, Philippine Sea. Extension: graben, new ocean floor. Volcanic arc on upper plate.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Specific gravity of gold is approximately:
questions.solution: Gold SG = 19.3. Very heavy. Gravity separation effective.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Cleavage in calcite:
questions.solution: Calcite (CaCO3) — double refraction. Effervesces in dilute HCl.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Shotcrete:
options: Sprayed concrete (dry or wet mix). Steel fiber reinforced. Applied to rock surface
questions.solution: Shotcrete: immediate support. Add accelerator. Wire mesh or fiber. 50-100mm thick.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Well interference:
options: Multiple wells → cone superposition. Additional drawdown. Reduced yield
questions.solution: Interference: s_total = Σs_i. Spacing wells to minimize. Constant rate vs constant drawdown.

questions.subject: Geology
questions.stem: Garnet porphyroblast with inclusion trail (Si/Se) indicates:
options: Timing of growth relative to deformation
questions.solution: Inclusion trails: show garnet grew during folding. Rotational vs non-rotational help determine syn-kinematic growth.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Crystal system of quartz:
questions.solution: Quartz: SiO2, hexagonal prisms terminated by pyramids. Piezoelectric.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A good index (guide) fossil should be:
options: Widespread but short-lived in geologic time
questions.solution: Wide distribution plus a short time range makes a fossil useful for correlation/dating.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Inertinite macerals origin:
options: Oxidized or charred plant material (fusain, charcoal)
questions.solution: Inertinite: fusinite, semifusinite, micrinite. High carbon, low volatile. May be wildfire products.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Esker:
options: Ridge of stratified sand/gravel deposited by subglacial stream
questions.solution: Esker: sinuous ridge. Glaciofluvial. Useful aggregate resource.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Spaceborne sensors — Landsat:
options: Landsat 8: OLI (9 bands, 30m) + TIRS (2 thermal, 100m). 16-day revisit. 185km swath
questions.solution: Landsat: 1972-2023. OLI bands: coastal, blue, green, red, NIR, SWIR1, SWIR2, pan (15m), cirrus.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Electrical resistivity tomography (ERT):
options: 2D/3D resistivity imaging. Dipole-dipole, Wenner, Schlumberger arrays. For contamination, cavity, archaeology
questions.solution: ERT: multi-electrode. Pseudo-section. Inversion. Time-lapse ERT for process monitoring.
